pain.label: The Problem
pain.title: Your best instructors spend half their week on paperwork.
pain.subtitle: Not tutoring. Not mentoring. Writing questions, grading papers, building Excel trackers — work that keeps your business running but doesn't generate a dime of revenue.

items.title: Content creation eats your margins
items.desc: A full-time curriculum developer costs $4,000-6,000/month, and 60% of their time goes to writing questions, building exams, and crafting explanations. The bigger you grow, the more you spend on work AI finishes in 10 seconds.

items.title: Retention plateaus without personalization
items.desc: Every student drills the same material. Top performers waste time on what they know. Strugglers get lost in content they're not ready for. Personalized review drives renewals — but doing it by hand is impossible at scale. Result: renewal rates stall at 60-70%.

items.title: Decisions based on gut feel, not data
items.desc: Which topic is the whole class struggling with? Which student is about to fall behind? You find out in the month-end spreadsheet — too late to intervene. Without real-time analytics, every curriculum change is a guess, and acquisition costs keep climbing.

features.label: What We Cover
features.title: Everything but the teaching.
features.subtitle: Content production, learning delivery, analytics — three integrated modules that run on autopilot, so your team focuses on growth.

items.title: AI Question Engine
items.subtitle: Adversarial pipeline · Production-ready output
items.desc: Three specialized AI agents — Author, Reviewer, and Classifier — work together on every question. The Author drafts. The Reviewer challenges for accuracy, clarity, and distractor quality. The Classifier validates difficulty and topic tags. Substandard output gets rejected and regenerated — up to 3 iterations — until it crosses a calibrated quality threshold. Compared to a single LLM call, our pipeline lifts the usable-question rate from roughly 60% to 85%+. Multiple choice, calculation, case analysis, essay — any format, any subject.
points: Author → Reviewer → Classifier: adversarial pipeline, up to 3 iterations
Auto quality gating with configurable thresholds, 85%+ usability
Every question type, any subject — discipline-aware generation

items.title: Memorix Adaptive Review
items.subtitle: Every student on their own optimal schedule
items.desc: Traditional spaced repetition uses fixed-interval algorithms (SM-2, FSRS). Memorix models forgetting with a Weibull distribution, capturing the steep-then-gradual decay of real human memory. After each answer, the algorithm updates a 20-dimensional personal parameter vector via online SGD with Nesterov momentum — every response fine-tunes the schedule. On 120,000 review logs from 500+ users, Memorix reduced prediction error by 13.7% vs FSRS v4.5 and improved long-term retention by 9.2%. Your students review exactly what they need, right before they'd forget it.
points: Weibull forgetting curves — more accurate than exponential or power-law models
Online SGD + Nesterov momentum — every answer refines the student's memory portrait
Brier Score calibration — forgetting predictions with statistical rigor
20-dimensional personalization — individual profiles, not population averages

items.title: Knowledge Dashboard
items.subtitle: Every student's mastery, visible in real time
items.desc: Color-coded knowledge graph: green for mastered, red for weak, gray for untouched. Click any node to see associated questions, mistake history, and resources. Students know exactly where they stand. Instructors pinpoint class-wide weak spots in seconds. You stop guessing and start making data-driven decisions about curriculum and intervention.
points: Interactive knowledge graph with zoom and pan
Mastery-based color coding — weak spots surface automatically
One-click navigation to questions, mistakes, and materials

items.name: Lin Jia

items.name: Wu Min

how.label: How It Works
how.title: Set up in minutes. Run on autopilot.
how.subtitle: No engineering team. No instructional designers. No IT.

steps.title: Pick your subjects
steps.desc: Register your business and select from pre-built knowledge frameworks — SAT, ACT, MCAT, LSAT, GRE, AP, USMLE, Bar Exam, and more. Or upload your own curriculum. The engine adapts immediately.

steps.title: AI generates your content
steps.desc: Open the Question Center, select topics and formats. The adversarial pipeline generates, reviews, and quality-checks every question. Your subject lead gives final approval — one click per batch.

steps.title: Students practice. You get the data.
steps.desc: Share a link or QR code. Students jump in on any device — no app to install. Auto-grading after every answer. The Knowledge Dashboard updates each student's mastery map in real time. You watch the business run.

steps.title: Custom Module Building
steps.desc: Advanced features like mock interviews, voice interaction, and AI Q&A can be activated on demand. Custom deployment available for Pro plans and above.

subjects.label: All Subjects
subjects.title: If it's tested, we cover it.
subjects.subtitle: The AI engine is an expert in how to write a good question — feed it any subject's material, and it generates in that discipline's voice. The platform doesn't care whether it's organic chemistry or constitutional law.
subjects.footer: Don't see your subject? Custom knowledge trees are built in minutes. We'll help you set it up.

items.q: Is UniMind only for specific subjects or exams?
items.a: No. The platform is completely subject-agnostic. Our AI engine generates questions in any language for any exam — SAT, ACT, MCAT, LSAT, GRE, AP, USMLE, Bar Exam, you name it. We have pre-built knowledge frameworks for the most common standardized tests, and you can upload your own curriculum in minutes. The adversarial pipeline works the same way regardless of subject.

items.q: How does the free trial work?
items.a: You get 7 days of full Growth-tier access — every feature, no restrictions, no credit card required. After the trial, your account automatically switches to your chosen plan. Upgrade or downgrade anytime. Your data stays intact regardless.

items.q: What makes Memorix different from Anki or spaced repetition?
items.a: Anki and similar tools use fixed-interval algorithms (SM-2, FSRS) that treat all learners the same. Memorix uses a Weibull-distribution forgetting model and online stochastic gradient descent to personalize 20 parameters per learner — every answer refines the schedule. On 120,000 review logs, Memorix reduced prediction error by 13.7% vs FSRS v4.5. It's not just 'when to review' — it's a learning-rate-adaptive scheduler tuned to each student.

items.q: How do my students access the platform?
items.a: They don't download anything. Share a link or QR code, and students jump in on any device — phone, tablet, laptop. The interface is mobile-optimized and works in any modern browser. Zero onboarding friction for students.

items.q: Can the AI generate questions in languages other than English?
items.a: Yes. The AI engine is multilingual — specify your output language and it generates accordingly. Our international users receive content in English, Spanish, Chinese, Arabic, French, and more.

Currently, billing is in CNY via Chinese payment processors. International billing (USD via Stripe) is on our near-term roadmap. For now, international customers can pay via wire transfer or contact our partnerships team.

items.q: How is UniMind different from platforms like Quizlet, Khan Academy, or Canvas?
items.a: Quizlet and Khan Academy give you pre-made content for a fixed set of subjects. Canvas is an LMS — it organizes content, it doesn't create it. UniMind produces and optimizes your teaching content with AI. The difference: questions are auto-generated, not manually entered or selected from a static library. Review is algorithm-personalized per student, not one-size-fits-all. Analytics are real-time and drillable to the topic level. We're the operations layer — you bring the teaching, we handle the rest.

items.q: I run a small tutoring center. Which plan should I start with?
items.a: Start with Free. 30 students and 30 AI generations per month let you validate the full workflow with a small group. When you're ready to scale, Starter (¥499/mo, ¥416/mo annual) removes the limits. Most centers upgrade within the first month once they see the time savings.

items.q: Can I integrate UniMind with my existing website or LMS?
items.a: Enterprise plan includes REST API access for integration. You can embed UniMind's question engine and review system into your existing platform. For custom integrations beyond the API, our solutions team can scope a tailored deployment — contact us through the demo request.
